get.summary: Active future scheduled maintenance windows + affected properties
get.description: SPEC §5.4 — feeds the dashboard's "Scheduled Maintenance" card.

schema.title: Response Get Maintenance Api V1 Maintenance Get

get.summary: Global search across properties, common areas, and network IDs
get.description: SPEC §5.4 / §8.3 — global search bar in the header / Cmd+K palette.

get.summary: Server-Sent Events stream of dashboard invalidations (SPEC §5.4)
get.description:
Long-lived SSE connection. Emits:

  • `hello`              — handshake on connect
  • `dashboard.invalidate` — client should re-fetch `/api/v1/dashboard`
  • `:heartbeat` comments  — keep-alives so proxies don't time out

Falls back to 30s polling on the client if the connection drops.

Per-user filtered dashboard.

Mock path returns the full set (dev convenience). Real path filters
properties to those the caller has access to.

get.summary: Connected-devices time series for stacked-area chart
get.description:
Critical, must-retain feature (SPEC §3 / README backend contract).

`ssid` omitted → totals (canonical `ssid=""` rows in `connected_device_count`).
`ssid` present → filtered series. Same timestamps drive both views.

get.summary: Distinct SSIDs seen for this property in the window
get.description: SPEC §7 — feeds the SSID dropdown on the Property Detail page.

schema.title: Response Get Property Ssids Api V1 Properties  Property Id  Ssids Get

get.description:
Property detail.

Mock path uses string IDs ("aks", "prk", …) so we can't pre-validate
via `require_property_access` (which expects int). The DB path enforces
per-property access inline.

post.summary: Generate a PDF WiFi network report for the property (SPEC §5.7)
post.description:
`POST /api/v1/properties/{id}/report` `{ssids: [...]}` → streams PDF.

Empty `ssids` means "include all" (matches the current Django app).

get.description:
`area_id` is the network_id (e.g. `AKS-001`). Mock-mode short-circuits;
real-mode enforces per-property access via the parent property.

post.summary: Force-check this network now (bypasses 1-hour rate limit)
post.description:
Hits eero `/network`, `/eeros`, `/devices` for this single area.
Updates cached state, writes a NetworkStatus row, refreshes EeroDevice
rows, writes ConnectedDeviceCount rows. Returns the post-call state.

schema.title: Response Post Area Check Api V1 Areas  Area Id  Check Post

post.summary: Force-check every network in this property now
post.description:
Iterates all common areas under the property and runs the full
refresh on each. Returns aggregate counts.

schema.title: Response Post Property Check Api V1 Properties  Property Id  Check Post
Response List Properties Api V1 Admin Properties Get
Response List Common Areas Api V1 Admin Properties  Property Id  Areas Get

post.description:
SPEC §6.1 — live eero validation. Operator confirms the network_id
+ name + eero count before saving.

schema.title: Response List Olt Cllis Api V1 Admin Clli Olt Get
Response List Seven Fifty Cllis Api V1 Admin Clli Seven Fifty Get
Response List Mdu Olt Map Api V1 Admin Mdu Olt Map Get

get.description: Distinct MDU names — drives the autocomplete on Add Property.

schema.title: Response List Mdu Olt Map Names Api V1 Admin Mdu Olt Map Names Get

post.description:
Replace the entire MDU↔OLT map with the contents of an uploaded
.xlsx (the SAG/FDH/EQUIP/SERVING_OLT export). The spreadsheet is the
source of truth — partial merges aren't supported.

AreaPreviewResponse.title: AreaPreviewResponse
AreaPreviewResponse.description:
Returned by `POST /api/v1/admin/areas/preview`.

Lets the operator confirm a `network_id` actually exists and belongs to
the right network before saving. The number of eero units is what they
use as a sanity check.

DeviceCountsResponse.title: DeviceCountsResponse
DeviceCountsResponse.description:
Contract for `GET /api/v1/properties/{id}/device-counts`.

Same shape is embedded into the dashboard hero chart so the frontend can
use a single component for both views.

MaintenanceWindow.title: MaintenanceWindow
MaintenanceWindow.description:
Light-weight wire shape for the dashboard's "Scheduled Maintenance"
card. Full admin shape lives in `schemas/maintenance.py`.

MduOltInfo.title: MduOltInfo
MduOltInfo.description:
Populated when the property's name matches an entry in the uploaded
MDU↔OLT map (admin → MDU Map upload).

ReportRequest.title: ReportRequest
ReportRequest.description:
Body of `POST /api/v1/properties/{id}/report`.

Empty `ssids` means "include all" — same UX as the current Django app
(zero checkboxes ⇒ all SSIDs).
